message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: bluEmber (rebranded to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar) (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: OpenTable ~896 diner reviews (legacy bluEmber listing, 4.3 stars); Tripadvisor 330 reviews 3.9 stars (now migrated to Desert Pearl page); Yelp 288 reviews (Yelp record marked 'CLOSED' under bluEmber name)
- Sentiment: mostly positive — fire-pit patio, views, live music and service consistently praised; food quality and value reviews are more mixed; some recent reviews specifically note exceptional multi-visit dining experiences
- Longevity: bluEmber operated for many years as the Omni Rancho Las Palmas signature restaurant; rebranded to Desert Pearl Bistro Bar in early 2026 [year-of-original-launch UNVERIFIED]
- Credentials: ["Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' outdoor dining recognition (legacy bluEmber) [UNVERIFIED year]","Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice (legacy bluEmber/Desert Pearl listing)","OpenTable 4.3-star aggregate from ~896 diners"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'date night Rancho Mirage', 'fire pit patio dining Rancho Mirage', 'Omni Rancho Las Palmas restaurant'. Does NOT consistently appear in 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' lists — Palm Springs Life, Tripadvisor and OpenTable curated 'best fine dining' roundups for Rancho Mirage lead with The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Oceana, Willie's Modern Fare, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar. Under the new Desert Pearl name, query surfacing is materially weaker than legacy bluEmber.
- Site structured data: Omni's hotel site exposes a Desert Pearl page at /dining/desert-pearl and still serves the legacy /dining/blu-ember URL. WebFetch was blocked (403) on both Omni dining pages and on Yelp/OpenTable, suggesting bot-hostile configuration that limits agent retrieval of canonical hours/menu/schema. Schema.org Restaurant markup on the Omni-owned pages [UNVERIFIED — could not retrieve HTML]. Menu lives at menus.omnihotels.com/htmlmenu/omnirancholaspalmasresortspa/bluember (still under the old slug) — strong signal that internal Omni systems haven't fully migrated the rebrand.
- Visibility gap score: 8
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the restaurant rebrand:

Desert Pearl Bistro Bar is formally known as bluEmber at Omni Rancho Las Palmas, blending mid-century modern style with California's desert charm. The restaurant is named after Palm Springs trailblazer Pearl McManus.

The Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort and Spa remodeled what was bluEmber, with plans to reopen it as Desert Pearl in January. Desert Pearl opened in the former bluEmber space at 41000 Bob Hope Drive in Rancho Mirage, offering both a breakfast and an "all-day" menu that includes items such as steak frites and vegetable couscous.

The rebrand appears to have been completed as of early 2026, with the new Desert Pearl restaurant now operating in the same location where bluEmber previously operated.

Based on the search results, here are some excellent Rancho Mirage restaurants suitable for a date night with fire pit and patio options:

## BluEmber at Omni Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa

This elegant restaurant offers fireside dining on their scenic patio overlook where you can enjoy roasted barramundi and braised short rib. You can cozy up by the fire pit and admire the spectacular mountains and lush fairways.

## Del Rey at Villa Royale

This intimate bar and eatery features an outdoor covered terrace equipped with a fire-pit for long nights under the desert moonlight. The menu features tapas-style fare inspired by Spanish and Mediterranean flavors.

## Ernie's at PGA West

This gastro-pub overlooks the famous Stadium and Nicklaus Tournament courses and features adventurous twists on familiar food, all made from scratch. The large patio provides outdoor living room furniture and table dining, with two gas fire pits that provide an area to relax, unwind and socialize.

These venues offer the combination of romantic date night ambiance with outdoor fire pit patios that make for an ideal desert evening experience.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining options in Rancho Mirage in 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Restaurants

**The Edge Steakhouse**
Located within The Ritz-Carlton, The Edge offers sweeping views of Rancho Mirage and crafts remarkable wagyu steaks, both premium hand-cut and bone-in meats as well as seafood paired with an international wine list. It's open Monday through Sunday from 5:00 p.m. to 10 p.m. with a smart casual dress code.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**
Established in 1978, Wally's Desert Turtle is a significant part of Rancho Mirage's culinary history and is celebrated as the pioneer of fine dining in the desert. It offers a refined, unhurried dining experience rooted in classic desert style with nightly entertainment, curated wines, and elegant California cuisine with signature soufflés.

**Willie's Modern Fare**
Restaurateur Willie Rhine pairs five-star hospitality with fine dining, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**Foxy's Kitchen + Bar**
A family-run spot from hospitality professionals, with steaks and osso buco as favorites, complemented by seafood, pastas, and vegan options.

**Si Bon**
Si Bon offers a delightful fusion of French and Belgian cuisine with a charming atmosphere, featuring classic French and Belgian dishes expertly prepared with creativity and flair.

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about bluEmber at Omni Rancho Las Palmas and its OpenTable reviews:

## OpenTable Rating and Review Summary

bluEmber at Rancho Las Palmas Resort & Spa is rated 4.3 stars by 896 OpenTable diners.

## Key Review Highlights

**Positive Feedback:**
- bluEmber boasts a "spectacular" new menu, with standout dishes like the Whipped Burrata and New York steak.
- Guests praise the "excellent and attentive" service and "beautiful surroundings."
- Guests enjoy "amazing drinks" and al fresco dining.

**Mixed Feedback:**
- While some find prices high, the "wonderful team" ensures a memorable experience.
- Guests highlight bluEmber's creative, seasonal menus that blend Californian and Southwestern flavors, a strong wine list, and friendly service that leans into resort-level hospitality.

## Restaurant Overview

bluEmber offers casual elegant Californian cuisine and serves as the resort's signature restaurant for an inventive spin on California New American cuisine. The restaurant features a sophisticated dining room and an award-winning patio where guests can enjoy food and wine pairings, as well as cocktails by fire pits with mountain views.

content.text: I have sufficient evidence. Yelp confirms 288 reviews and "CLOSED" status under bluEmber name. The menu still lives at the legacy bluember slug. Palm Springs Life "Best of the Best" outdoor dining recognition is independently confirmed. Tripadvisor 330 reviews/3.9 stars confirmed. OpenTable 4.3/896 reviews confirmed for bluEmber legacy listing. "Best fine dining" lists confirm The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar lead and bluEmber/Desert Pearl is absent.

confirms: OpenTable: ~896 diner reviews at 4.3 stars on legacy bluEmber listing — confirmed via OpenTable search snippet
Tripadvisor: 330 reviews at 3.9 stars on the page now titled 'Desert Pearl' (Tripadvisor URL still uses d1028964 for the legacy listing, ranked #17 of 68) — confirmed
Yelp: 288 reviews with the page marked 'CLOSED' under the bluEmber name — confirmed via Yelp listing title 'BLUEMBER - CLOSED - 272 Photos & 288 Reviews'
Rebrand from bluEmber to Desert Pearl in early 2026 — confirmed via Coachella Valley Independent and Omni/AAA/Visit Greater Palm Springs listings
Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' outdoor dining recognition for legacy bluEmber — confirmed via third-party coverage citing the award
Surfaces strongly for 'date night Rancho Mirage' / 'fire pit patio' queries (legacy bluEmber still appears in Locale Magazine date-night list, OpenTable scenic-view and fireplace lists)
Does NOT appear in 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' curated lists led by The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, Foxy's Kitchen + Bar (Si Bon also surfaces) — confirmed
Menu still hosted at menus.omnihotels.com/htmlmenu/omnirancholaspalmasresortspa/bluember under old slug — confirmed via search result
Omni dining page returns HTTP 403 on WebFetch — confirmed bot-hostile config; canonical schema cannot be retrieved by agents
contradicts: Claim implies Tripadvisor reviews 'migrated to Desert Pearl page' — technically the same Tripadvisor restaurant ID (d1028964) was renamed; reviews didn't migrate, the listing was relabeled in place. Minor framing nuance.

Claim that 'Oceana' appears in Rancho Mirage best-fine-dining lists is not supported by my searches — top lists surface The Edge, Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare, Foxy's, and Si Bon. Oceana did not appear in Palm Springs Life/Tripadvisor/OpenTable Rancho Mirage rankings I retrieved (Oceana is in Palm Desert, not Rancho Mirage).
flagged_as_unverified: Year of original bluEmber launch — already marked UNVERIFIED in the brief; could not pin down opening year (resort opened in 1950s but bluEmber-branded launch date not surfaced)
Year of Palm Springs Life 'Best of the Best' outdoor dining recognition — award confirmed but specific year not retrievable
Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice award for the listing — could not independently confirm via search snippets
Presence of schema.org Restaurant JSON-LD on Omni dining pages — Omni site returns 403 to agents; could not inspect HTML for structured data

Sentiment specifics (fire-pit patio, views, live music praise vs. mixed food/value reviews) — OpenTable snippet supports positive service/patio sentiment and notes high prices, broadly consistent but not exhaustively verified per-platform
input.notes: Core visibility claims (review counts, ratings, rebrand timing, fine-dining list absence, bot-hostile fetch behavior, legacy menu slug) all independently confirmed. Two small corrections: 'Oceana' should not be in the Rancho Mirage fine-dining competitor set (it's in Palm Desert), and the Tripadvisor situation is a rename-in-place rather than a migration. Visibility-gap score of 8 and anchor-reference tier are defensible given the strong legacy footprint plus weak new-brand surfacing and 403-blocked canonical pages.
